userdoc.notice: Anchors batched H-IoT device readings on-chain as IPFS hashes and         emits on-chain alerts when vital sign thresholds are crossed. This contract is the blockchain anchor for the H-IoT layer described in the SC-BHIoT paper.  Raw vitals are NEVER stored on-chain; only the IPFS CID of an encrypted vitals batch is stored.  Threshold events are fully on-chain so the frontend can subscribe and show live alerts. NEW CONTRACT — does not exist in v1. Architecture:  IoT Device (Python sim) → MQTT broker → Node.js backend    → encrypt + upload to IPFS → get CID    → call logVitalsBatch() on this contract    → smart contract checks thresholds and emits AlertTriggered if needed    → frontend WebSocket receives the event and shows the alert dashboard

params.ipfsHash: IPFS CID of the encrypted vitals JSON batch

params.threshold: The threshold constant that was crossed
params.value: The actual sensor reading that triggered the alert

checkHeartRate(uint256).notice: Returns whether a heart rate reading crosses any threshold.         The backend can call this as a view before deciding to triggerAlert.

deregisterDevice(address).notice: Remove a device from the whitelist (lost / replaced / retired)

getAlertsByPatient(address).notice: Returns all alert IDs for a patient

getLatestLog(address).notice: Returns the latest log for a patient (most recent vitals)

getLogsByPatient(address).notice: Returns all vitals log IDs for a patient

getThresholds().notice: Returns threshold constants as a struct for frontend display

logVitalsBatch(string).notice: Log a batch of vitals readings anchored by their IPFS hash.         Called by the Node.js IoT ingestion service after encrypting         and uploading the readings to IPFS.

registerDevice(address,address).notice: Register a new IoT device and associate it with a patient.         The device address is the wallet used by the backend IoT bridge.

triggerAlert(address,string,uint256,uint.notice: Trigger a threshold alert.         Called by the Node.js backend when it detects a threshold         crossing in the raw sensor data BEFORE uploading to IPFS.         The alert is stored on-chain for an immutable audit record.

documentation.text:
@title  IoTDataLogger
 @notice Anchors batched H-IoT device readings on-chain as IPFS hashes and
         emits on-chain alerts when vital sign thresholds are crossed.
 This contract is the blockchain anchor for the H-IoT layer described in
 the SC-BHIoT paper.  Raw vitals are NEVER stored on-chain; only the IPFS
 CID of an encrypted vitals batch is stored.  Threshold events are fully
 on-chain so the frontend can subscribe and show live alerts.
 NEW CONTRACT — does not exist in v1.
 Architecture:
  IoT Device (Python sim) → MQTT broker → Node.js backend
    → encrypt + upload to IPFS → get CID
    → call logVitalsBatch() on this contract
    → smart contract checks thresholds and emits AlertTriggered if needed
    → frontend WebSocket receives the event and shows the alert dashboard
